resources.string: current bands: UNKNOWN — refusing to swap
You\'re currently on %1$s bands. To swap to %2$s, the app needs the %2$s firmware blob. Get it once — after that, every swap is automatic.

Your phone is reporting bands that match neither US nor stock. The app refuses to touch a phone in this state — you\'d brick the modem. Make sure this is a Qin F21 (pre-v3) and that nothing else has modified the bands.
Loaded into app storage. You can delete the file you picked from your phone.

This will:

1. Back up your current %1$s bands (~1–2 minutes).
2. Flash %2$s bands directly to the modem partitions.
4. Reboot the device immediately (sysrq).

Close any other apps with unsaved work first — the reboot is a forced shutdown and skips the normal shutdown.

After the reboot you must re-provision IMEI / Bluetooth MAC / WiFi MAC using the mtk-imei-switcheroo-app (link in Info), otherwise the new modem will boot with whatever placeholder values are in the flashed nvram.

This will WIPE your current %1$s bands and overwrite the IMEI, Bluetooth MAC, and WiFi MAC with placeholder values from the flashed nvram. There is NO BACKUP. You cannot revert without re-downloading the %1$s blob and flashing it back.

If you continue, the app will skip the backup step entirely and go straight to the eMMC unlock + flash + sysrq reboot. After the reboot you must re-provision IMEI / Bluetooth MAC / WiFi MAC with the mtk-imei-switcheroo-app (link in Info).
The flash finished but the device didn\'t auto-reboot. Power-cycle the device manually for the new bands to take effect.
Root not available. This app needs Magisk root.
Refusing to operate: current bands match neither US nor stock. See Info for details.
That file doesn\'t look like a valid F21 bands blob.
Swap failed: %1$s\n\nYour previous bands\' backup is in app storage. See Info for recovery instructions.

EXCLUSIVELY for rooted DuoQin F21 prior to v3.

v3 has a RED charging port and serial number prefix F21PMQC25 — running this app on v3 will SOFT-BRICK THE MODEM.

If you're not sure, don't use this app.

Swap to <target> bands — the safe path. Backs up your current bands to app private storage (~1–2 min), flashes the new bands, sysrq-reboots. The backup lets you swap back later without re-downloading. The backup also still has your old IMEI, bt and wifi mac addresses for the next time you swap.

Flash <target> — NO BACKUP — the destructive path. Skips the backup step entirely and goes straight to unlock + flash + reboot. Saves a minute or two. If you want to return to the bands you had before, you have to re-download that blob.

Both paths - if this is the first flash (flashing from download) overwrite IMEI, BT MAC, and WiFi MAC with placeholder values — re-provision with the IMEI switcher app afterwards either way.
Modifying an IMEI is illegal in some jurisdictions. You are responsible for checking your local laws.

After flashing the first time, your IMEI / BT MAC / WiFi MAC are placeholder values from the downloaded nvram. The mtk-imei-switcheroo-app re-provisions them. This downloads and installs the latest version of the app.
Checks GitHub for a newer release and installs if one exists.

The live on-device band-swap procedure (eMMC CMD0 reinit, USR_WP unlock, CMD29 cascade, sysrq-b coherency trick) was reverse-engineered and validated end-to-end by alltechdev. Without that work this app would still be a brick generator. Massive thanks.
